full_text_rationale: Full text proposes ReCreate, an experience-driven framework that stores and retrieves task-agent trajectories, scaffold updates, tools, and static memory entries across iterations, with evaluation on SWE-Bench Verified, DA-Code, DS-1000, AppWorld, and other benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 012; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Zhezheng Hao, Hong Wang, Jian Luo, Jianqing Zhang, Yuyan Zhou, Qiang Lin, Can Wang, Hande Dong, Jiawei Chen
titulo: ReCreate: Reasoning and Creating Domain Agents Driven by Experience
formato_armazenamento: ReCreate-Environment stores current scaffold, full interaction trajectories, submitted artifacts, execution and evaluation results, codebase/database/sandbox context, evidence indexes, scaffold diffs, reusable tools, and static memory entries with lessons, failure modes, repair heuristics, and tool-use tips.
framework_agente: ReCreate bi-level domain-agent creation framework with a task agent, ReCreate-Agent scaffold optimizer, evidence retriever, creation router, hierarchical domain update, static memory bank, and tool creation
llm_utilizado: gpt-5-mini for task agents; claude-4.5-opus for ReCreate-Agent scaffold updates
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; multi-domain comparison; scaffold-generation baseline comparison; action-level ablation; reasoning-level ablation; cost comparison; temperature sensitivity; case study
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-Bench Verified Django and SymPy subsets, DA-Code data wrangling, DA-Code machine learning, DA-Code statistical analysis, DS-1000 NumPy, DS-1000 Pandas, DS-1000 Matplotlib, MATH Algebra, MATH Number Theory, MATH Counting and Probability, AppWorld Normal, AppWorld Challenge
metricas_desempenho: Pass rate or testing score, domain average score, action distribution, ablation scores, optimization cost in USD, temperature-sensitivity performance
metricas_cl: Iterative scaffold evolution over up to two optimization loops, development/test split, static memory accumulation, generated tools, domain-level update aggregation, cost of scaffold optimization across domains
baseline_comparado: CoT, Step-Back Abstraction, Self-Refine, LIVE, OPRO, ExpeL, ADAS, AgentSquare, ReCreate without creation router, ReCreate without domain update
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: The Django case study shows ReCreate evolving a minimal seed scaffold into prompt templates, memory interfaces, static memory entries, and custom tools; the paper does not report total memory-entry counts over all domains.
custo_por_tarefa: Figure 6 reports scaffold-optimization cost by domain; ReCreate reduces cost relative to ADAS by roughly 36% to 82%.
resultado_principal: Across 13 benchmark subsets, ReCreate obtains an average score of 66.15, higher than the strongest listed competing averages such as ExpeL at 60.62 and LIVE at 60.57.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE subsets, ReCreate reaches 60.19 on Django and 63.64 on SymPy, exceeding the best baseline in each row. It also improves over ADAS and AgentSquare averages by more than seven points.
custo_vs_baseline: Compared with ADAS, ReCreate is reported to reduce scaffold-optimization cost by roughly 36% to 82% because it uses interaction evidence and smaller development sets rather than repeated candidate evaluation.
limitacoes: ReCreate optimizes textual and code-level scaffolds but not infrastructure or execution environments, and it does not update base model parameters.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on small sampled development sets, benchmark-specific scaffold design spaces, use of gpt-5-mini and claude-4.5-opus, Docker sandbox execution, cost-estimation assumptions, and whether domain-agent creation from minimal seeds generalizes beyond the tested domains.
